deviations.description: The process model does not include any activity or gateway that terminates the phone company process if it exceeds 30 days. The new requirement introduces an obligation to terminate the process after 30 days, which is not enforced in the current process.
process_model_reference: entire phone company process (all activities after 30 days, including 'Send SIM card')

deviations.description: The modality has changed from permission to obligation, requiring the process to terminate if it exceeds 30 days. The process model allows the process to continue beyond 30 days without termination, directly violating the new obligation.
process_model_reference: entire phone company process (all activities after 30 days, including 'Send SIM card')

deviations.description: The compliance pattern now requires the execution of a termination activity when the process duration exceeds 30 days. The process model does not contain any such termination activity, resulting in a violation.
process_model_reference: entire phone company process (all activities after 30 days, including 'Send SIM card')

deviations.description: The dimension has shifted from time to control flow, obligating a specific control flow action (termination) that is not present in the process model.
process_model_reference: entire phone company process (all activities after 30 days, including 'Send SIM card')

deviations.description: The required activity has changed from sending the SIM card after 30 days to terminating the process after 30 days. The process model continues with normal activities instead of terminating, violating the new requirement.
process_model_reference: entire phone company process (all activities after 30 days, including 'Send SIM card')

deviations.description: The temporal constraint modification is a consequence of the change in compliance pattern and activity; the new requirement is already captured by the previous changes.
